channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: DreamHack Birmingham Confirmed to Return in 2027
item.content:encoded:
DreamHack Birmingham was apparently an immediate success, as the event will return to the English Midlands next year.

After making its UK debut, DreamHack has confirmed the LAN event will return to the NEC from April 2 to April 4 2027.

This year’s event was claimed to have drawn in over 54,000 visitors from 65 different countries, as fans gathered to play games and watch esports tournaments with almost $1.7m (£1.3m) in prize money given out in total.

DreamHack returning next year is a major boost for the British gaming community, as DreamHack fills the hole left by Insomnia Gaming Festival.

DreamHack Birmingham Delivered Exciting Esports Action

One of the headline events of the weekend was the ESL One Birmingham Dota 2 tournament, as 16 of the world’s best teams competed at the major event with a $1m (£749k) prize pool.

Tundra was crowned ESL One Birmingham Champion as British pro Matthew ‘Ari’ Walker got to celebrate in front of an overjoyed home crowd.

And at the same time, there was yet another electric crowd lapping up the Call of Duty League Major 2 tournament.

The live crowd brought the energy and had people excited about CDL again.

Adam Adamou, ceo of Toronto Koi’s parent company Overactive Media, posted on social media how delighted he was with the event and stressed:

“Hopefully it’s becoming obvious [to the CDL] that CoD is best in front of a live audience.”

“That’s the heart of this game as an esport. Think big. Increase to Eight Majors plus Champs. That means more games and a longer season. There’s no reason we need to end the season in June,” he added.

The event ended with a classic showdown between old rivals FaZe and OpTiC as FaZe Vegas won the CDL Major 2 and claimed the $150k (£114k) grand prize.

If you attended DreamHack Birmingham, share your experiences with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And there could be more for British CoD fans to look forward to with a CDL Champs return to Europe on the cards.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Wario & Waluigi Appearances Teased For The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
item.content:encoded:
The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is shaping up to be one of 2026’s biggest events. New actors and roles have slowly been drip-fed in the build-up to the film’s release. In front of a watching world, main cast members, Charlie Day and Chris Pratt, may have indicated that Wario and Waluigi are appearing in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie.

You can’t deny how good the casting choices were for The Super Mario Bros. Movie. Charlie Day was born to play Luigi, Chris Pratt quelled doubts about playing Mario, Jack Black is a monstrous Bowser, and there really isn’t a bad role anywhere.

With Galaxy looking stacked, even more iconic Mario characters could be introduced.

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie Could Add Wario & Waluigi

On an episode of The Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon, Charlie Day and Chris Pratt appeared to promote The Super Mario Galaxy Movie.

In a fun segment, Day and Pratt were given novelty Mario buttons to press in response to questions from Fallon about the movie. You press the red shell if the answer is no, whereas a yes warrants a ‘?’ Block press.

Fallon ran through a few questions, but got to a very interesting one, which elicited a curious response from the actors:

“Wario and Waluigi make an appearance.”

Day hit the red shell, and Pratt pressed the block—giving us a non-committal answer, and you’d think there’s only one reason why they’d opt to do this…

Waluigi and Wario are in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie!

Don’t take this seriously, of course. While the actors could be teasing the world, knowing this could be a question, this might be another reason to go and watch the movie.

If Waluigi and Wario do appear, it could simply be a post-credits cameo setting up a third movie. Check out the full segment here.

Are Wario and Waluigi all but locked in for The Super Mario Galaxy Movie? Or is this an elaborate ploy? Let us know what you think via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out The Super Mario Galaxy Movie voice actor for Star Fox’s Fox McCloud being revealed and Donald Glover talking about playing Yoshi for more news.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Halloween Multiplayer Gameplay Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
New details about the multiplayer gameplay for the upcoming Halloween game by IllFonic have been revealed.

Halloween is a title based on the wildly popular horror franchise featuring the iconic Michael Myers. The game was revealed last year and is all set to arrive in September 2026. The upcoming game features a 1v4 asymmetrical horror experience, and the developers have just revealed more details on the multiplayer features.

Play as Michael Myers or the Residents of Haddonfield in Halloween Multiplayer

IllFonic’s new blog post discusses the multiplayer gameplay details for Halloween. In this experience, players can choose between two roles: they can step into the shoes of the methodical killer, Michael Myers, and hunt down their targets, or they can take on the role of a resident trying to rescue themselves and their neighbors from the boogeyman.

Michael Myers Abilities in Halloween

When playing as the villain in Halloween multiplayer, players will have to kill all residents across town; however, there are some Special Targets you need to go after for better rewards. Michael Myers has abilities that can be used to locate, terrorize, and execute civilians. Killer Sense allows the character to be more aware of his surroundings and of others’ proximity. The character can build Stalk levels to help identify and track targets or to open more execution options.

Then you have Shape Jump, an ability to amplify the character’s mobility, allowing Michael to move through shadows stealthily. There’s also Shape Dash, which gives the character the power to lunge forward with “violent momentum.” However, Shape Jump only works in darkness outside a civilian’s line of sight.

Resident Abilities in Halloween

The game not only provides players with the power to hunt as the villain but also with tools to survive as a civilian. The civilian’s role will be to find other Haddonfield residents and guide them to safety. Each civilian has been designed with unique stats, special traits, and signature personalities.

When exploring, civilians will reveal new POIs, resident NPCs, lootable containers, hiding places, telephones, possible escape routes, and more. Talking to residents will also provide civilians with additional tools, such as requesting police backup, commands for finding secure hiding places, or companionship when running from the boogeyman.

The lootable containers in Halloween multiplayer feature weapons, distractions, and key items that will aid the players’ survival. Moreover, even in death, civilians will be able to help others by returning as “a Sheriff’s Deputy or the unrelenting Dr. Loomis.” The developers have also stated, “While he can’t be killed, with enough community resistance, the proper equipment, and sheer manpower, he can be detained and shipped back to Smith’s Grove Sanitarium.”

In other news, Xbox’s latest partner preview event featured new gameplay and closed beta details for The Expanse: Osiris Reborn. Additionally, the second title for PlayStation Plus’s April 2026 monthly games has been revealed. What are your thoughts on Halloween’s multiplayer details? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Halo: Combat Evolved campaign details surfacing online. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: First Halo: Combat Evolved Campaign Prequel Mission Details Surface in New Claim
item.content:encoded:
Halo: Campaign Evolved is set to bring the first-ever Halo campaign back to life. A 2026 release beckons, and if you can’t wait for something more official, a reported leak has shed some light on the new content.

It’s still disappointing that there will be no multiplayer element in Halo: Campaign Evolved. On the plus side, Halo Studios is putting a lot of heart and soul into the campaign, and doing so involves new features, content, and expands on the Halo lore.

Halo: Campaign Evolved Prequel Mission Content Reportedly Revealed

Halo Studios announced early on that Halo: Campaign Evolved would have three never-before-seen missions, serving as a prequel to the main game.

With the game set to release in 2026, we still haven’t heard anything about the missions. However, Rebs Gaming claims to have a source that has gotten the lowdown on this content, and Rebs Gaming believes the report to be accurate—but this exclusive leak needs to be taken with a huge pinch of salt.

Here’s what Rebs Gaming had to say about the prequel missions (from their source):

“They include an ODST Helljumper who will reignite the old rivalry between Spartans and ODSTs, which started during the early days of Master Chief’s Spartan training program.”

The references to other Halo games don’t stop there, as Rebs’ source also mentions: “There’s a small homage to Halo: Reach in these new missions.”

The leak adds more details:

“I’m also told the new missions feature a never-before-seen Covenant faction with models built entirely from scratch, which is where Halo Studios’ leadership decided to invest their resources into. This new Covenant variant has a much more religious appearance than what the fans are used to, and its way of acting differs a bit, especially regarding technology.

Finally, Rebs says that Brutes are also going to feature in the prequel missions as well. Again, this is all far from confirmed, so be ready for any potential changes or release plans that don’t align with this leak.

Check out the full video right here.

Whether you’re a long-time Halo fan going back to the OG game, or you’re still relatively green on your Halo lore, let us know what you think of this leak through the Insider Gaming Discord. Is any of this likely? Would you be excited for these potential prequel missions?

For more Xbox news, read about the Xbox Games Showcase being announced for June 2026, and take a look at unseen Grand Theft Auto 4 dev kit assets being leaked for other gaming news.
